_comment: Strict seccomp profile for high-risk tools (e.g. code_tool). IMPORTANT: with urunc this profile filters the HOST-SIDE monitor process (QEMU), NOT syscalls inside the guest VM. The guest is already isolated by the hypervisor boundary; this profile is defence-in-depth that shrinks the monitor's host attack surface by denying syscalls a VMM never legitimately needs. defaultAction ALLOW + explicit denials keeps QEMU functional while blocking privilege-escalation / host-manipulation calls. See docs/adr/0003-seccomp-hardens-the-monitor.md.

syscalls.comment: Deny module loading, host mount/namespace manipulation, kernel control, ptrace, eBPF, and keyring access. A QEMU monitor needs none of these; denying them shrinks the host attack surface if the monitor is compromised.
